title: writeRecord helper core, per-record issue schema, and `issue create` CLI
description: Stand up the single write path for issues end-to-end: author a per-record issue schema (one file = one issue, not the collection wrapper), widen the issue `id` pattern and the `prd` reference pattern (and the PRD schema's own `id`) to accept legacy `EXEC-NNN`/`PRD-NNN` OR UUIDv7, and build the `writeRecord` helper that mints a UUIDv7, rejects any caller-supplied id, derives a frozen slug from the title, validates against the per-record schema with a bundled ajv, serializes canonically, and writes atomically to `.excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json`. Expose it as `to-execution issue create`. Demoable: create an issue from the CLI and get a schema-valid uuid-keyed file.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution issue create --title ...` writes `.excn/issues/<uuid>-<slug>.json` with a valid UUIDv7 id and a slug derived (sanitized, lowercased) from the title
The helper rejects a record that arrives with an `id` field on create
A per-record issue schema exists and the helper validates against it; the legacy collection schema is retired or clearly scoped to archives
Issue `id` pattern, the `prd` reference pattern, and the PRD schema `id` pattern accept legacy ids OR UUIDv7
ajv is a declared package dependency and the validation path survives a fresh `npm install` (no host-root/pruned-module breakage) — sprint-6 lesson
All literals (uuid version, filename pattern, slug rules) are named constants, not inline — sprint-5 lesson
Serialization is library parse→mutate→stringify so malformed output (trailing comma) is structurally impossible — sprint-6 lesson
Write is temp-file + rename; a mid-write failure leaves no partial file
`.excn/.gitignore` does not catch `.excn/issues/*.json`; per-issue files stay git-tracked (ADR-0005, phantom-wipe class)
Helper unit tests cover: valid mint, supplied-id rejection, slug derivation, atomic write, schema-invalid rejection
